methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mbodi AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $3M+ to date. The valuation landscape in Robotics & Manufacturing comprises 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though narrow peer-stage data limits direct comparable company analysis.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using the $3M total capital raised as a proxy for current intrinsic value and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to Series A, this method implies a valuation of $7.5M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied; while 11 robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the broader comp set, fewer than three datapoints met the narrow filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier positioning. Reliance on all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given heterogeneity within the sector.

The triangulated valuation range, supported by one viable method, is $6M to $9M, with a median of $8M. This translates to approximately a 2.7x markup on capital deployed. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer benchmarking at the Seed stage and reliance on a stage-progression multiplier that may not reflect Mbodi AI's specific commercial traction, technical differentiation, or market conditions. Validation of this range should incorporate more granular operational metrics once available.
